title: cheilanthes horridula
intro: Cheilanthes horridula, also known as the rough rock fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cheilanthes horridula include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Cheilanthes horridula can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems can be used in a variety of herbal preparations, including salves and ointments.
